Terms and Conditions|$नयम और शत
1. General Terms and Conditions-
1.1 This contract is governed by the General Terms and Conditions, conditions stipulated to this Product/Service as provided in the Marketplace.
1.2 This Contract between the Seller and the Buyer, is for the supply of the Goods and/ or Services, detailed in the schedule above, in accordance with the General Terms and
Conditions (GTC) unless otherwise superseded by Goods / Services specific Special Terms and Conditions (STC) and/ or BID/Reverse Auction Additional Terms and Conditions
(ATC), as applicable
2. Buyer Added Bid Specific Terms and Conditions-
2.1 Generic: 
OPTION CLAUSE: The Purchaser reserves the right to increase or decrease the quantity to be ordered up to 25 percent of bid quantity at the time of placement of contract. The
purchaser also reserves the right to increase the ordered quantity by up to 25% of the contracted  quantity during the currency of the contract at the contracted rates. Bidders
are bound to accept the orders accordingly.
2.2 Warranty: 
Warranty period of the supplied products shall be 5 years from the date of final acceptance of goods or after completion of installation, commissioning & testing of goods (if
included in the scope of supply), at consignee location. OEM Warranty certificates must be submitted by Successful Bidder at the time of delivery of Goods. The seller should
guarantee the rectification of goods in case of any break down during the guarantee period. Seller should have well established Installation, Commissioning, Training,
Troubleshooting and Maintenance Service group in INDIA for attending the after sales service. Details of Service Centres near consignee destinations are to be uploaded along
with the bid.
2.3 Forms of EMD and PBG: 
Bidders can also submit the EMD with Account Payee Demand Draft in favour of
S N Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences
payable at
Kolkata
.
Bidder has to upload scanned copy / proof of the DD along with bid and has to ensure delivery of hardcopy to the Buyer within 5 days of Bid End date / Bid Opening date.
2.4 Forms of EMD and PBG: 
Successful Bidder can submit the Performance Security in the form of Account Payee Demand Draft also (besides PBG which is allowed as per GeM GTC). DD should be made in
favour of
S N Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences
payable at
Kolkata
. After award of contract, Successful Bidder can upload scanned copy of the DD in place of PBG and has to ensure delivery of hard copy to the original DD to the Buyer within 15

1. General Terms and Conditions-
1.1 This contract is governed by the General Terms and Conditions, conditions stipulated to this Product/Service as provided in the Marketplace.
1.2 This Contract between the Seller and the Buyer, is for the supply of the Goods and/ or Services, detailed in the schedule above, in accordance with the General Terms and
Conditions (GTC) unless otherwise superseded by Goods / Services specific Special Terms and Conditions (STC) and/ or BID/Reverse Auction Additional Terms and Conditions
(ATC), as applicable
2. Buyer Added Bid Specific Terms and Conditions-
2.1 Warranty: 
Warranty period of the supplied products shall be 5 years from the date of final acceptance of goods or after completion of installation, commissioning & testing of goods (if
included in the scope of supply), at consignee location. OEM Warranty certificates must be submitted by Successful Bidder at the time of delivery of Goods. The seller should
guarantee the rectification of goods in case of any break down during the guarantee period. Seller should have well established Installation, Commissioning, Training,
Troubleshooting and Maintenance Service group in INDIA for attending the after sales service. Details of Service Centres near consignee destinations are to be uploaded along
with the bid.
2.2 Turnover: 
Bidder Turn Over Criteria: The minimum average annual financial turnover of the bidder during the last three years, ending on 31st March of the previous financial year, should
be as indicated in the bid document. Documentary evidence in the form of certified Audited Balance Sheets of relevant periods or a certificate from the Chartered Accountant /
Cost Accountant indicating the turnover details for the relevant period shall be uploaded with the bid. In case the date of constitution / incorporation of the bidder is less than 3
year old, the average turnover in respect of the completed financial years after the date of constitution shall be taken into account for this criteria.
2.3 Turnover: 
OEM Turn Over Criteria: The minimum average annual financial turnover of the OEM of the offered product during the last three years, ending on 31st March of the previous
financial year, should be as indicated in the bid document. Documentary evidence in the form of certified Audited Balance Sheets of relevant periods or a certificate from the
Chartered Accountant / Cost Accountant indicating the turnover details for the relevant period shall be uploaded with the bid. In case the date of constitution / incorporation of
the OEM is less than 3 year old, the average turnover in respect of the completed financial years after the date of constitution shall be taken into account for this criteria. In case
of bunch bids, the OEM of CATEGORY RELATED TO primary product having highest bid value should meet this criterion.
2.4 Service & Support: 
Availability of Service Centres: Bidder/OEM must have a Functional Service Centre in the State of each Consignee's Location in case of carry-in warranty. (Not applicable in case of
goods having on-site warranty). If service center is not already there at the time of bidding, successful bidder / OEM shall have to establish one within 30 days of award of
contract. Payment shall be released only after submission of documentary evidence of having Functional Service Centre.
2.5 Service & Support: 
Dedicated /toll Free Telephone No. for Service Support : BIDDER/OEM  must have  Dedicated/toll Free Telephone No. for Service Support.
2.6 Service & Support: 
Escalation Matrix For Service Support : Bidder/OEM must provide  Escalation Matrix of Telephone Numbers for Service Support.
2.7 Buyer Added Bid Specific ATC:

2.8 Past Project Experience: 
Proof for Past Experience and Project Experience clause:  For fulfilling the experience criteria any one of the following documents may be considered as valid proof for meeting
the experience criteria:a. Contract copy along with Invoice(s) with self-certification by the bidder that service/supplies against the invoices have been executed.b. Execution
certificate by client with contract value.c. Any other document in support of contract execution like Third Party Inspection release note, etc.Proof for Past Experience and Project
Experience clause: For fulfilling the experience criteria any one of the following documents may be considered as valid proof for meeting the experience criteria:a. Contract copy
along with Invoice(s) with self-certification by the bidder that service/supplies against the invoices have been executed.b. Execution certificate by client with contract value.c. Any
other document in support of contract execution like Third Party Inspection release note, etc.
2.9 Past Project Experience: 
The Bidder / OEM {themselves or through reseller(s)}, should have executed project for supply and installation / commissioning of same or similar Category Products during
preceding 3 financial years (i.e. current year and three previous financial years) as on opening of bid, as per following criteria:
 
(i) Single order of at least 35% of estimated bid value; or
(ii) Two orders of at least 20% each of estimated bid value; or
(iii) Three orders of at least 15% each of estimated bid value.
Satisfactory Performance certificate issued by respective Buyer Organization for the above Orders should be uploaded with bid. In case of bunch bids, the Category related to
primary product having highest bid value should meet this criterion
2.10 Forms of EMD and PBG: 
Bidders can also submit the EMD with Account Payee Demand Draft in favour of
S N Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences
payable at
Kolkata
.
Bidder has to upload scanned copy / proof of the DD along with bid and has to ensure delivery of hardcopy to the Buyer within 5 days of Bid End date / Bid Opening date.
2.11 Forms of EMD and PBG: 
Successful Bidder can submit the Performance Security in the form of Account Payee Demand Draft also (besides PBG which is allowed as per GeM GTC). DD should be made in
favour of
S N Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences
payable at
Kolkata
. After award of contract, Successful Bidder can upload scanned copy of the DD in place of PBG and has to ensure delivery of hard copy to the original DD to the Buyer within 15
days of award of contract.
2.12 OEM: 
IMPORTED PRODUCTS: In case of imported products, OEM or Authorized Seller of OEM should have a registered office in India to provide after sales service support in India. The
certificate to this effect should be submitted.
2.13 Scope of Supply: 
Scope of supply (Bid price to include all cost components) : Supply Installation Testing Commissioning of Goods and Training of operators and providing Statutory Clearances
required (if any)

TERMS AND CONDITIONS:  
01. Purchase Order duly signed by the authorised signatory and issued on the form prescribed by TTIPL shall alone be valid 
02. Details of PO should be quoted on all correspondences, advices challans and invoices etc. relating to the PO 
03. The PO must be acknowledged within a week from the date of its receipt. In case the acknowledgement is not received within the said period,
it shall be deemed that the PO has been fully accepted by the Supplier to effect supplies in accordance with the terms and conditions thereunder.
04. Any specific terms and conditions agreed between TTIPL and Supplier shall override these Terms and Conditions to the extent of conflict only.
05. Price indicated in the PO are firm and can be revised only by mutual consent in writing.
06. All payments due under this PO are subject to deduction of tax at source at the rates prevailing at the time of payment.
07. For the goods produced out of TTIPL's materials, no scrap allowance is permissible unless specified in the PO. If the amount of scrap exceeds the
limit indicated in the PO, supplier shall be charged for the materials and the labour cost for such excess scrap.
08. The goods should be dispatched through TTIPL's designated carrier, clearly indicating the description,quantities and weight of each consignment.
Delivery note and copy of the Invoice and necessary documents shall accompany the goods.
09. Packing method for both returnable/one way packaging shall be as per agreed terms. Supplier shall properly secure and pack the goods against
loss, damage or corrosion in transit. Supplier shall be responsible for transit damage. Each case must be properly identified and contents of each
case must be indicated separately in the delivery note.
10. Delivery of goods shall be complete on their physical delivery at TTIPL's work or any other designated place; title in delivered goods shall pass on
to TTIPL on final inspection for their defects and acceptance of the goods by TTIPL.
11. Transportation cost would be paid by TTIPL to supplier along with price of goods if it is agreed between TTIPL and supplier and mentioned in PO.
However, all supplies to TTIPL shall be made on payment of full freight cost to the carrier I.e On 'Freight Paid' basis and not on 'Freight To-Pay'
basis.
12. Supply shall be as per the schedule indicated by TTIPL from time to time. Goods delivered in excess of quantities ordered and scheduled by TTIPL
will not be accepted and all costs in respect of the same shall be to the account of and at the risk and responsibility of the Supplier.
13. In case of force majeure events and other circumstances beyond the control of TTIPL, TTIPL may suspend the PO or amend the delivery dates to
such extent as TTIPL may consider necessary, without incurring any liability.
14. All goods shall match in every respect of the description, specification, and previous approved samples submitted (if any) and shall be subjected 
to TTIPL's inspection and approval within a reasonable time after delivery and if rejected shall be held at Supplier's risk. Supplier shall make
necessary arrangements to take the rejected goods from TTIPL within a reasonable time. Failure to do so will absolve TTIPL of any responsibility in
regard to the rejected goods not collected by the supplier.
15  All goods supplied to TTIPL shall be subject to the normal warranty offered by Supplier or special warranty agreed between TTIPL and supplier.
16. The sale or use of Supplier's products shall not infringe any Indian or Foreign trademark, trade name, patent or registered design and the
Supplier's shall indemnify and keep TTIPL indemnified against all acts, judgement, decrees, costs, claims, demands and expenses resulting from
an actual or alleged infringement and shall at its own expenses defend TTIPL or assist in the defence of any suit or action that may be brought
against TTIPL in that connection. 
17. All parts manufactured to the specification or drawings given by TTIPL shall be sold only to TTIPL and shall not be sold as spares in the market. 
Infringement of this condition will make the Supplier liable for such damages as may be claimed by TTIPL and also will result in forthwith
cancellation of the PO 
18. Supplier shall not disclose any business information of or part with any intellectual property of TTIPL to any third party under any circumstances.
19. Authorised representative of TTIPL shall be allowed to enter Supplier's facility during working hours to inspect their facility, goods, materials or 
any property provided/financed by TTIPL.
20. If the delivery dates set forth in the contract are not met due to any reason attributable to Supplier except under standard Force Majeure 
conditions, TTIPL shall have the right to claim liquidated damages (L.D) of zero point one(0.1) percent, per day, on the portion of the goods 
due for delivery. However, the aggregate of L.D. amount would not exceed ten (10) percent of the price of goods due for delivery. In the
event of the supplier to deliver the goods even after lapse of maximum L D Period, TTIPL shall have the right to cancel the PO, without
payment of any compensation to Supplier. 
21. In cases where the input material is supplied by TTIPL, the material accounting statement shall be provided by the Supplier before the third
day of every month. 
22. The prices indicated in the PO are firm and can be revised only by mutual negotiation and prior consent of TTIPL
23. Performance against the PO shall be considered due to Bidadi in the State of Karnataka for the purpose of jurisdiction. All disputes,
which may arise in connection with this PO shall be resolved in the courts in Bangalore shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any such disputes. 
24. All goods are subject to the physical verification of the quantity and quality and their acceptance is subject to the Receipt Rejection and Line
Rejection process and final approval by the Quality Control Team of TTIPL. The decision of TTIPL in this matter will be final and binding
25. Failure to comply with any of the conditions under this PO gives the right to TTIPL to cancel the PO for the balance of item and quantities
undelivered and without prejudice to proceed against the Supplier in respect of the defaults of the Suppliers
regarding their GST compliance.
7. The vendor must communicate their GST compliance rating on a quarterly basis, and also shall furnish details as may be requested by TTIPL 
collected from TTIPL and file correct returns.  
8. The Vendor shall pay interest to TTIPL @1.5% for each month or part of the month towards the delay, in case of vendor fails to remit the GST 
remittance/ non-remittance of GST amount defaulted by the vendors in respect of their past bills raised on TTIPL until the same is rectified along with
9. While making vendor payments, in addition to any other statutory deductions TTIPL reserves the right to withhold an amount equivalent to the short 
interest as aforesaid.
